SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter

Class AbstractListPayloadSplitter<T>

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter.AbstractPayloadSplitter<List<T>,T>
com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter.AbstractListPayloadSplitter<T>
Type Parameters:
T - slice type — the element type of the List<T> the annotated method returns
All Implemented Interfaces:
PayloadSplitter<List<T>,T>
public abstract class AbstractListPayloadSplitter<T> extends AbstractPayloadSplitter<List<T>,T>
AbstractPayloadSplitter specialised for the most common scatter/gather shape: a composite payload that is a List<T> of slices of type T.

Fixes T = List<T> and R = T and supplies working defaults for three of the four hooks, so the minimum a subclass must provide is keyArgsForSlice(Object, StoreContext) — the slice key. The supplied defaults are:

  • splitIntoSlices(List) — identity: each list element is one slice.
  • keyArgsToRecover(List, RecoverContext) — a single arg-group (List.of(args)), which is correct only for the findAll() / no-id-args case. Every id-based scenario must override it.
  • mergeSlices(List, List) — returns the recovered slices as-is (nulls kept positionally) and flattens the per-slice arg-groups into one aggregate arg list. Override to deduplicate, drop nulls, or reject partial recoveries.

When to override keyArgsToRecover(java.util.List<java.lang.Object>, com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter.RecoverContext<java.util.List<T>>):

Method shape → what to override
MethodOverride needed
findAll()none — default single-group is correct
findByIdsIn(List<Long> ids)split ids into one group per id
findByIdsInAndActiveAndRegion(List<Long>, Boolean, String)split ids; ignore the filter args for keying
findByStringIdsIn(String csv)split the CSV into one group per id
findByStringIdsInAndActiveAndRegion(String, Boolean, String)split the CSV; ignore the filter args
Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractListPayloadSplitter

      public AbstractListPayloadSplitter(Class<T> clazz)
      Parameters:
      clazz - the slice type T; used to stamp every per-slice RecoverContext so the delegate recovers the right type

  • Method Details

    • keyArgsForSlice

      protected abstract List<Object> keyArgsForSlice(T payload, StoreContext<List<T>> context)
      Derives the store-key args for a single slice. The only mandatory hook — typically List.of(payload.getId()). See AbstractPayloadSplitter.keyArgsForSlice(R, com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter.StoreContext<T>).
      Specified by:
      keyArgsForSlice in class AbstractPayloadSplitter<List<T>,T>
      Parameters:
      payload - the individual slice being stored
      context - the composite store context (full payload + original method args)
      Returns:
      key args identifying payload; typically a single-element list of the entity id

    • splitIntoSlices

      protected List<T> splitIntoSlices(List<T> payloads)
      Breaks the composite payload into individual slices on the store path.

      One StoreContext is built per returned slice. For a List<R> composite this is the identity (each element is a slice) — see splitIntoSlices(List).

      Identity split: the composite list is the slice list, so each element becomes one stored slice.

      Specified by:
      splitIntoSlices in class AbstractPayloadSplitter<List<T>,T>
      Parameters:
      payloads - the composite payload returned by the annotated method
      Returns:
      the slices to store individually, in order

    • keyArgsToRecover

      protected List<List<Object>> keyArgsToRecover(List<Object> args, RecoverContext<List<T>> context)
      Splits the aggregate method args into one arg-group per slice on the recover path.

      Each returned List<Object> drives exactly one recover/find call on the slice delegate and must derive the same key AbstractPayloadSplitter.keyArgsForSlice(Object, StoreContext) produced when storing.

      Shapes by scenario (assuming the slice key is the entity id):

      • findAll() — no args: return a single group List.of(args) (the default in AbstractListPayloadSplitter); the delegate recovers all slices by name.
      • findByIdsIn(List<Long> ids): return one group per id — ids.stream().map(List::of).toList().
      • findByStringIdsIn(String csv): split the CSV, then one group per id.
      • with extra filter args (active, region, ...): ignore the filters for keying — they are not entity identity — and split only the id portion.

      Default is for the findAll() / no-id-args case only: returns a single arg-group wrapping the original args, producing one recover call (the delegate fetches all slices by name). For any id-based method (findByIdsIn, findByStringIdsIn, with or without extra filters) override this to return one group per entity id as a List<List<Object>>.

      Specified by:
      keyArgsToRecover in class AbstractPayloadSplitter<List<T>,T>
      Parameters:
      args - the original aggregate method args (possibly empty for findAll())
      context - the composite recover context (failover, args, cause)
      Returns:
      one arg-group per slice to recover; an empty list suppresses recovery

    • mergeSlices

      protected MergeResult<List<T>> mergeSlices(List<T> payloads, List<List<Object>> args)
      Re-assembles the recovered slices into the composite payload on the recover path.

      Owns the null/partial-recovery policy: a slice payload may be null (cache miss, expired, or timed-out). Choose to keep nulls positionally, drop them, deduplicate, or reject the whole composite — this method has the domain knowledge to decide. It also owns how the per-slice args are aggregated back into the composite args.

      Default merge: returns the recovered slices as the composite list (nulls kept positionally) and flattens the per-slice arg-groups into a single aggregate arg list. Override to deduplicate, drop null slices, or reject partial recoveries — see the null-policy discussion on AbstractPayloadSplitter.mergeSlices(java.util.List<R>, java.util.List<java.util.List<java.lang.Object>>).

      Specified by:
      mergeSlices in class AbstractPayloadSplitter<List<T>,T>
      Parameters:
      payloads - recovered slice payloads in slice order; entries may be null
      args - the per-slice arg-groups in the same order as payloads
      Returns:
      the merged composite payload plus aggregated args, wrapped in a MergeResult
